text:
MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY / LIQUID BIOPSY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INSTITUTION: [Academic Medical Center — Division of Molecular Pathology & Genomics]
DEPARTMENT: Molecular Diagnostics Laboratory
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------
Specimen ID:        MP-LBX-[ACCESSION]
Specimen Type:      Plasma-derived cell-free DNA (cfDNA); peripheral blood
Collection Method:  Peripheral venous blood draw; two 10 mL Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes collected
Volume Collected:   20 mL whole blood (two tubes)
Sample Quality:     Acceptable. cfDNA yield: 18.4 ng/mL plasma. Plasma volume processed: 4.2 mL.
Assay Platform:     Guardant360 (Guardant Health, Redwood City, CA) — next-generation sequencing-based liquid biopsy, 74-gene panel
Ordering Provider:  [Attending Medical Oncologist, Thoracic Oncology]
Clinical Indication: EGFR-mutant (L858R) Stage IV non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma), right upper lobe primary, on osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily x 14 months. New imaging evidence of intracranial metastases (3 lesions on MRI brain) and systemic progression (right upper lobe mass increased from 1.8 cm to 2.7 cm; new right paratracheal lymphadenopathy). Liquid biopsy obtained to evaluate for acquired resistance mechanisms prior to modification of systemic therapy.

CLINICAL CONTEXT
----------------
Patient is a 64-year-old woman of East Asian (Cantonese) descent with a history of Stage IIIB (T3 N3 M0) NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, harboring EGFR L858R mutation (exon 21) and co-mutations TP53 p.R248W, SMARCA4 p.R1192H (per initial FoundationOne CDx NGS). ALK IHC negative, ROS1 IHC negative, PD-L1 TPS 5% at diagnosis. TMB 4 mut/Mb. MET low-level amplification on initial NGS (copy number ratio 2.4, below high-amplification threshold). Patient was initiated on osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line monotherapy for unresectable Stage IIIB EGFR-mutant NSCLC; achieved a partial response at 3 months (primary tumor 47% reduction) and maintained partial/stable response through 12 months. At 14 months, imaging demonstrates systemic and CNS progression as noted above. This cfDNA assay is submitted to characterize acquired resistance mechanisms.

REFERENCE METHOD / ASSAY DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------
Guardant360 is a validated Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA)-certified, College of American Pathologists (CAP)-accredited liquid biopsy assay. The assay analyzes single nucleotide variants (SNVs), indels, fusions, copy number amplifications, and microsatellite instability in 74 cancer-related genes from circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) isolated from plasma. Analytical sensitivity for point mutations is approximately 0.1% variant allele frequency (VAF) in samples with adequate ctDNA yield. Results are reported for alterations with VAF >= 0.15% or meeting platform-defined thresholds for copy number changes and fusions. Variants of uncertain significance are reported where applicable.

DETECTED ALTERATIONS
---------------------

1. EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G), Exon 21
   Classification:   Pathogenic — Sensitizing/Oncogenic Mutation (Driver)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF):   1.2%
   Interpretation:   This is the founding oncogenic driver mutation in this patient's NSCLC. Detection of L858R at VAF 1.2% in ctDNA confirms ongoing ctDNA shedding from EGFR-mutant tumor and is consistent with active disease at the time of progression. Reduction from the presumed tumor fraction at diagnosis is consistent with prior treatment effect; however, the increase in measurable disease on imaging correlates with detectable ctDNA elevation.

2. EGFR p.T790M (c.2369C>T), Exon 20
   Classification:   Pathogenic — Acquired Resistance Mutation
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF):   0.6%
   Interpretation:   EGFR T790M is a well-characterized acquired resistance mutation in patients treated with first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs (erlo, Iressa, afat). Its presence in a patient treated with osi (a third-generation, T790M-active TKI) is ATYPICAL and warrants careful interpretation. Possible explanations include: (a) a pre-existing low-level T790M sub-clone present at diagnosis that was not detected by tissue-based PCR (cobas threshold ~1%) or initial NGS (FoundationOne CDx, below detection), which may have been present at sub-clonal frequency and not appreciably suppressed by osi monotherapy; (b) T790M arising as a secondary event in a heterogeneous resistance context; or (c) a co-occurring resistance mechanism in which T790M represents one component of polyclonal acquired resistance. Given the low VAF of 0.6% and the clinical context, T790M alone is unlikely to be the dominant resistance driver. Correlation with tissue re-biopsy is strongly recommended. Molecular tumor board review recommended.
   Clinical Note:    T790M at this low VAF in the context of Tagrisso therapy should not be interpreted as a definitive indication for further T790M-targeting; osi achieves robust T790M suppression. Additional resistance mechanisms should be presumed.

3. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T), Exon 7
   Classification:   Pathogenic — Tumor Suppressor Loss-of-Function (Co-mutation; present at baseline)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF):   1.1%
   Interpretation:   This TP53 hotspot mutation was identified in the original FoundationOne CDx report (VAF 42.1% in tissue) and is concordantly detected here in ctDNA, confirming clonal persistence. The lower VAF in plasma compared to tissue reflects the dilutional effect of non-tumor cfDNA. TP53 p.R248W is a dominant negative gain-of-function mutation associated with more aggressive tumor behavior and is a known co-mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC that may contribute to therapeutic resistance.

NEGATIVE FINDINGS (CLINICALLY RELEVANT)
-----------------------------------------
The following alterations were NOT detected above the assay's limit of detection:

- EGFR Exon 19 deletion:   Not detected
- EGFR p.C797S (Exon 20):  Not detected
  [Note: EGFR C797S is the most common mechanism of Tagrisso resistance in the T790M-positive setting; its absence may suggest an alternative resistance pathway or that T790M is not the dominant mechanism here.]
- EGFR Exon 20 insertion:  Not detected
- KRAS mutation (all codons):   Not detected [expected in EGFR-mutant NSCLC; consistent with known mutual exclusivity]
- ALK fusion:   Not detected
- ROS1 fusion:  Not detected
- RET fusion:   Not detected
- MET amplification:   Not detected above reporting threshold [note: low-level MET amplification was identified on original tissue NGS at copy number ratio 2.4; cfDNA copy number analysis has lower sensitivity than tissue-based methods for low-level amplifications; absence here does not exclude low-level MET amplification in tumor]
- BRAF p.V600E:  Not detected
- PIK3CA mutation:  Not detected
- ERBB2 (HER2) amplification or mutation:  Not detected
- MET Exon 14 skipping mutation:  Not detected
- NTRK1/2/3 fusion:  Not detected
- PTEN mutation:  Not detected
- Microsatellite Instability (MSI-High):  Not detected

ADDITIONAL ASSAY METRICS
--------------------------
Tumor Mutational Burden (cfTMB):   Not reportable from this sample (cfTMB assessment requires adequate tumor fraction; current ctDNA fraction is at the lower range for cfTMB calculation; tissue-based TMB from prior FoundationOne CDx was 4 mutations/Mb [Low])
ctDNA Fraction (estimated):   Low (~1–2%, estimated from EGFR L858R VAF with correction for ploidy assumptions)
Assay QC:   PASS. All internal controls within acceptable ranges. Sufficient cfDNA input for variant calling.

SAMPLE ADEQUACY NOTE
----------------------
This sample contains detectable ctDNA (cfDNA yield 18.4 ng/mL; EGFR L858R detected at VAF 1.2%). While the overall ctDNA fraction is low, the assay's analytical sensitivity enables reliable detection of variants above 0.15% VAF. Low-level copy number changes (amplifications at low copy number ratios) and gene fusions may have reduced sensitivity at this ctDNA level. A negative result for any specific alteration does not exclude its presence at sub-threshold levels in the tumor.

INTERPRETATION AND SUMMARY
----------------------------
This liquid biopsy reveals a complex ctDNA profile at the time of confirmed radiographic progression on osi monotherapy. The key findings are:

1. Confirmed persistence of the founding EGFR L858R driver mutation (VAF 1.2%), consistent with active EGFR-mutant disease.
2. Detection of acquired EGFR T790M at low VAF (0.6%). This is unusual in the context of third-generation TKI (Tagrisso) therapy, which is T790M-active. The biological significance is uncertain; this may represent: (a) a pre-existing sub-clonal T790M not suppressed by osi at this low level; (b) a minor component of polyclonal resistance not serving as the dominant mechanism. The absence of EGFR C797S, which would be expected to co-occur with T790M as a tandem osi resistance mechanism, argues against T790M being the dominant driver.
3. TP53 p.R248W concordantly detected, consistent with baseline characterization.
4. No dominant classical osi resistance mechanism identified by this liquid biopsy alone (no C797S, no amplification detected, no bypass fusion). The molecular basis of progression remains incompletely defined from ctDNA at this ctDNA fraction.

CLINICAL CONSIDERATIONS
------------------------
Given the low ctDNA fraction and atypical/ambiguous resistance profile, tissue re-biopsy of the progressing lesion (right upper lobe mass and/or right paratracheal lymph node) with comprehensive repeat NGS is strongly recommended to:
  - Confirm or exclude high-level MET amplification (not detectable by cfDNA at this tumor fraction but suspected given low-level baseline amplification)
  - Identify additional bypass track mutations (PIK3CA, BRAF, RAS pathway) not detectable in plasma at this sensitivity
  - Characterize the full repertoire of acquired resistance mechanisms for treatment planning
  - Correlate tissue T790M frequency with plasma findings

Molecular tumor board consultation is recommended prior to next-line treatment selection.

DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY
-------------------
ACQUIRED EGFR T790M RESISTANCE MUTATION DETECTED at low allele frequency (VAF 0.6%) in plasma ctDNA. EGFR L858R (founding driver) and TP53 p.R248W (co-mutation) also detected. No dominant alternative resistance mechanism identified by liquid biopsy. Clinical correlation and tissue confirmation strongly recommended.

Electronic signature: [Molecular Pathologist, MD/PhD, Molecular Diagnostics]
Reviewing pathologist attestation: Results reviewed, interpreted, and reported by a board-certified pathologist with subspecialty training in molecular genetic pathology.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This assay was performed at Guardant Health (CLIA # [XX-XXXXXXX]; CAP Accredited). Report reviewed and interpreted at [Institution] Division of Molecular Pathology.
Final report. No amendments.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
